forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KTBNHSR4EVTVJ35MGGD510R2
+- 01KTBN8CQDYD52W5MGG4ZYKNN7
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8e80'
+project: local-review
+title: 'Author first-class quality-dimension rules: duplication, reuse, data-driven, dead-code'
+---
+## What
+Promote the exact problems the user complains about — duplicated/copy-pasted code, ignoring shared libraries, needless helper methods, hardcoded instead of data-driven solutions — into first-class, dedicated review rules (rules-as-data) so each gets its own fleet agent with an explicit probing mandate. Today these are buried as Layer-2 footnotes a single agent rushes; the existing `builtin/validators/code-quality/rules/no-hard-code.md` only catches the narrow "return 42 to pass a test" case.
+
+Author/expand rules under `builtin/validators/` (new no-`trigger` format, `match.files` globs for source code), grouped to the design's dimensions:
+- **duplication-reuse**: flag verbatim/near-verbatim blocks (mandate: run `find duplicates` on every changed block) AND missed reuse of existing shared functions/libraries (mandate: `search symbol` for an existing function before accepting a new one). Two blocks differing only by a value = one function with an argument. Flag needless helper methods that wrap a single call site.
+- **data-driven-design**: flag hardcoded literals/`match`/`if`-chains over a known set whose arms differ only in constants — these are tables, not control flow. Repeated literals → named constant/config. Calibrated by rule-of-three (two is coincidence, three is a pattern); no speculative abstraction.
+- **dead-code**: added/changed symbol with empty inbound callgraph that isn't an entry point/export/test (mandate: `get callgraph` inbound + `get blastradius`); orphaned modules, unreachable branches, commented-out code.
+- Each rule body states its severity (duplication/dead-code = blocker, per the current skill), the probe it must run, and the carve-outs (rule-of-three, exported APIs, entry points) so the fleet/verifier don't over-flag.
+- Migrate the still-relevant existing code-quality rules (cognitive-complexity, function-length, naming-consistency, no-commented-code, no-magic-numbers, missing-docs) into their dimensions; delete any made redundant.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] Rules for duplication-reuse, data-driven-design, and dead-code exist in the new format with explicit per-rule probe mandates and severity, mapped to the design dimensions.
+- [ ] Existing code-quality rules are re-homed to dimensions; no rule still carries `trigger`.
+- [ ] Each rule names the carve-outs that prevent over-flagging (rule-of-three, entry points/exports/tests).
+- [ ] A loader test confirms the new rule sets parse and match a `.rs` file under the expected dimensions.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Loader/parse test: every authored `VALIDATOR.md` + `rules/*.md` parses and resolves to its dimension; `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` green.
+- [ ] Fixture review test (with the engine + a scripted agent): a temp file containing an obvious copy-paste block and a hardcoded if-chain → the duplication and data-driven dimensions produce findings (proves the rule text drives the agent to the right probe).
+
+## Workflow
+- Content-authoring task grounded in code. Read `builtin/skills/review/SKILL.md` Layer 2 and the existing `builtin/validators/code-quality/rules/*.md` first; reuse their wording where good. Pair each rule with the code_context probe that confirms it. Depends on the format task (no-`trigger` schema) and the design (dimension names).
\ No newline at end of file

Two blocks differing only by a value = one function with an argument. Flag needless helper methods that wrap a single call site.
-- **data-driven-design**: flag hardcoded literals/`match`/`if`-chains over a known set whose arms differ only in constants — these are tables, not control flow. -- [ ] A loader test confirms the new rule sets parse and match a `.rs` file under the expected dimensions.
-
-## Tests
-- [ ] Loader/parse test: every authored `VALIDATOR.md` + `rules/*.md` parses and resolves to its dimension; `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` green.
-- [ ] Fixture review test (with the engine + a scripted agent): a temp file containing an obvious copy-paste block and a hardcoded if-chain → the duplication and data-driven dimensions produce findings (proves the rule text drives the agent to the right probe).
-
-## Workflow
-- Content-authoring task grounded in code. Read `builtin/skills/review/SKILL.md` Layer 2 and the existing `builtin/validators/code-quality/rules/*.md` first; reuse their wording where good. Pair each rule with the code_context probe that confirms it. Depends on the format task (no-`trigger` schema) and the design (dimension names).

+Split the monolithic `code-quality` validator into focused validators under `builtin/validators/` (new no-`trigger` format; frontmatter `name`, `description` = the agent mandate, `match.files` globs, `severity`, `probes`):
+- **duplication** (severity blocker, `probes: [find_duplicates, search_symbol]`): flag verbatim/near-verbatim blocks AND missed reuse of existing shared functions/libraries. Two blocks differing only by a value = one function with an argument. Flag needless helper methods wrapping a single call site.
+- **data-driven** (`probes: [search_symbol]`): flag hardcoded literals and `match`/`if`-chains over a known set whose arms differ only in constants — these are tables, not control flow. Repeated literals → named constant/config. Rule-of-three calibration (two is coincidence, three is a pattern); no speculative abstraction.
+- **dead-code** (severity blocker, `probes: [get_callgraph, get_blastradius]`): added/changed symbol with empty inbound callgraph that isn't an entry point/export/test; orphaned modules, unreachable branches, commented-out code.
+- Each validator's rule bodies state severity, the probe to run, and the carve-outs (rule-of-three, exported APIs, entry points/tests) so the fleet/verifier don't over-flag.
+- Re-home the still-relevant existing code-quality rules (cognitive-complexity, function-length, naming-consistency, no-commented-code, no-magic-numbers, missing-docs) into their own focused validators; delete any made redundant.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] Rules for duplication-reuse, data-driven-design, and dead-code exist in the new format with explicit per-rule probe mandates and severity, mapped to the design dimensions.
-- [ ] Existing code-quality rules are re-homed to dimensions; no rule still carries `trigger`.
-- [ ] Each rule names the carve-outs that prevent over-flagging (rule-of-three, entry points/exports/tests).
-- [ ] A loader test confirms the new rule sets parse and match a `.rs` file under the expected dimensions.
+- [ ] `duplication`, `data-driven`, and `dead-code` validators exist in the new format, each a single focused concern with `description` (mandate), `match.files`, `severity`, and `probes`.
+- [ ] Existing code-quality rules are re-homed to focused validators; no validator carries `trigger`; the monolithic multi-concern `code-quality` set is gone.
+- [ ] Each validator names the carve-outs that prevent over-flagging.
+- [ ] A loader test confirms the new validators parse, expose their `probes`, and match a `.rs` file.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Loader/parse test: every authored `VALIDATOR.md` + `rules/*.md` parses and resolves to its dimension; `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` green.
-- [ ] Fixture review test (with the engine + a scripted agent): a temp file containing an obvious copy-paste block and a hardcoded if-chain → the duplication and data-driven dimensions produce findings (proves the rule text drives the agent to the right probe).
+- [ ] Loader/parse test: every authored `VALIDATOR.md` + `rules/*.md` parses, `probes` resolve to real code_context ops; `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` green.
+- [ ] Fixture review test (engine + scripted agent): a temp file with an obvious copy-paste block and a hardcoded if-chain → the `duplication` and `data-driven` validators each produce findings (proves the rule text + probes drive the agent correctly).

 ## Workflow
-- Content-authoring task grounded in code. Read `builtin/skills/review/SKILL.md` Layer 2 and the existing `builtin/validators/code-quality/rules/*.md` first; reuse their wording where good. Pair each rule with the code_context probe that confirms it. Depends on the format task (no-`trigger` schema) and the design (dimension names).
\ No newline at end of file

Pair each validator with the code_context probe that confirms it. Depends on the format task (no-`trigger`, `probes` schema) and the design (validator catalog).

 ## What
-Promote the exact problems the user complains about — duplicated/copy-pasted code, ignoring shared libraries, needless helper methods, hardcoded instead of data-driven solutions — into first-class FOCUSED VALIDATORS (rules-as-data), so each gets its own fleet agent with an explicit probe mandate. +Promote the exact problems the user complains about — duplicated/copy-pasted code, ignoring shared libraries, needless helpers, hardcoded instead of data-driven — into first-class FOCUSED VALIDATORS (rules-as-data), one concern per validator so each gets its own focused fleet agent. Today these are buried as Layer-2 footnotes a single agent rushes.

-Split the monolithic `code-quality` validator into focused validators under `builtin/validators/` (new no-`trigger` format; frontmatter `name`, `description` = the agent mandate, `match.files` globs, `severity`, `probes`):
-- **duplication** (severity blocker, `probes: [find_duplicates, search_symbol]`): flag verbatim/near-verbatim blocks AND missed reuse of existing shared functions/libraries. Two blocks differing only by a value = one function with an argument. Flag needless helper methods wrapping a single call site.
-- **data-driven** (`probes: [search_symbol]`): flag hardcoded literals and `match`/`if`-chains over a known set whose arms differ only in constants — these are tables, not control flow. Repeated literals → named constant/config. Rule-of-three calibration (two is coincidence, three is a pattern); no speculative abstraction.
-- **dead-code** (severity blocker, `probes: [get_callgraph, get_blastradius]`): added/changed symbol with empty inbound callgraph that isn't an entry point/export/test; orphaned modules, unreachable branches, commented-out code.
-- Each validator's rule bodies state severity, the probe to run, and the carve-outs (rule-of-three, exported APIs, entry points/tests) so the fleet/verifier don't over-flag.
-- Re-home the still-relevant existing code-quality rules (cognitive-complexity, function-length, naming-consistency, no-commented-code, no-magic-numbers, missing-docs) into their own focused validators; delete any made redundant.
+Split the monolithic `code-quality` validator into focused validators under `builtin/validators/` (new format: `name`, `description` = the agent mandate, `match.files` globs, `severity`, `probes` — using the catalog names `callers`/`duplicates`/`similar`; most validators have NO probe and that's correct):

+| Validator | Concern | `probes` | severity |
+|-----------|---------|----------|----------|
+| `duplication` | verbatim/near-verbatim copied blocks | `[duplicates]` | blocker |
+| `reuse` | reimplements an existing shared fn/library instead of calling it; needless helper wrapping one call site | `[similar]` | warning |
+| `data-driven` | hardcoded literals / `match`/`if`-chains over a known set that should be a table; repeated literals → named constant | (none) | warning |
+| `dead-code` | added symbol with no inbound callers, not an entry point/export/test; orphaned modules, unreachable branches, commented-out code | `[callers]` | blocker |
+| `complexity` | cognitive complexity (re-homed) | (none) | warning |
+| `function-length` | over-long functions (re-homed) | (none) | warning |
+| `naming` | naming consistency (re-homed) | (none) | nit/warning |
+| `no-commented-code` | commented-out code (re-homed) | (none) | nit |
+| `magic-numbers` | unexplained literals (re-homed) | (none) | nit |
+| `missing-docs` | missing doc comments (re-homed) | (none) | nit |
+
+- Each validator's rule bodies state the carve-outs that prevent over-flagging: `reuse`/`data-driven` honor rule-of-three (two is coincidence, three is a pattern) and no speculative abstraction; `dead-code` exempts entry points, exported public API, and tests.
+- Probe names must be the catalog's (`duplicates`/`similar`/`callers`); NO `search_symbol` or `get_blastradius`. In-file validators declare no probes.
+- Delete the monolithic multi-concern `code-quality` set once its rules are re-homed; `no-hard-code` ("return 42 to pass a test") goes to the `tests`/test-integrity validator (safety-migration task), not here.
+
 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `duplication`, `data-driven`, and `dead-code` validators exist in the new format, each a single focused concern with `description` (mandate), `match.files`, `severity`, and `probes`.
-- [ ] Existing code-quality rules are re-homed to focused validators; no validator carries `trigger`; the monolithic multi-concern `code-quality` set is gone.
-- [ ] Each validator names the carve-outs that prevent over-flagging.
+- [ ] `duplication`, `reuse`, `data-driven`, `dead-code` exist as separate focused validators with the probes/severities above; `reuse` is split out from `duplication`.
+- [ ] The six re-homed quality validators exist; the monolithic `code-quality` set is gone; no validator carries `trigger`.
+- [ ] Every declared probe is a real catalog name (`duplicates`/`similar`/`callers`); in-file validators declare none.
+- [ ] Each validator names its carve-outs.
 - [ ] A loader test confirms the new validators parse, expose their `probes`, and match a `.rs` file.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Loader/parse test: every authored `VALIDATOR.md` + `rules/*.md` parses, `probes` resolve to real code_context ops; `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` green.
-- [ ] Fixture review test (engine + scripted agent): a temp file with an obvious copy-paste block and a hardcoded if-chain → the `duplication` and `data-driven` validators each produce findings (proves the rule text + probes drive the agent correctly).
+- [ ] Loader/parse test: every authored `VALIDATOR.md` + `rules/*.md` parses; declared probes pass `probe_exists`; `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` green.
+- [ ] Fixture review test (engine + scripted agent): a temp file with an obvious copy-paste block, a reimplementation of an existing util, and a hardcoded if-chain → the `duplication`, `reuse`, and `data-driven` validators each produce a finding (proves rule text + probes drive the agents correctly).

 ## Workflow
-- Content-authoring task grounded in code. Read `builtin/skills/review/SKILL.md` Layer 2 and the existing `builtin/validators/code-quality/rules/*.md` first; reuse their wording. Pair each validator with the code_context probe that confirms it. Depends on the format task (no-`trigger`, `probes` schema) and the design (validator catalog).
\ No newline at end of file

reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -6,40 +6,27 @@
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: '8e80'
 project: local-review
-title: 'Author focused validators: duplication, reuse, data-driven, dead-code (+ re-homed quality)'
+title: 'Author first-class focused validators: duplication, reuse, data-driven, dead-code'
 ---
 ## What
-Promote the exact problems the user complains about — duplicated/copy-pasted code, ignoring shared libraries, needless helpers, hardcoded instead of data-driven — into first-class FOCUSED VALIDATORS (rules-as-data), one concern per validator so each gets its own focused fleet agent. Today these are buried as Layer-2 footnotes a single agent rushes.
-
-Split the monolithic `code-quality` validator into focused validators under `builtin/validators/` (new format: `name`, `description` = the agent mandate, `match.files` globs, `severity`, `probes` — using the catalog names `callers`/`duplicates`/`similar`; most validators have NO probe and that's correct):
-
-| Validator | Concern | `probes` | severity |
-|-----------|---------|----------|----------|
-| `duplication` | verbatim/near-verbatim copied blocks | `[duplicates]` | blocker |
-| `reuse` | reimplements an existing shared fn/library instead of calling it; needless helper wrapping one call site | `[similar]` | warning |
-| `data-driven` | hardcoded literals / `match`/`if`-chains over a known set that should be a table; repeated literals → named constant | (none) | warning |
-| `dead-code` | added symbol with no inbound callers, not an entry point/export/test; orphaned modules, unreachable branches, commented-out code | `[callers]` | blocker |
-| `complexity` | cognitive complexity (re-homed) | (none) | warning |
-| `function-length` | over-long functions (re-homed) | (none) | warning |
-| `naming` | naming consistency (re-homed) | (none) | nit/warning |
-| `no-commented-code` | commented-out code (re-homed) | (none) | nit |
-| `magic-numbers` | unexplained literals (re-homed) | (none) | nit |
-| `missing-docs` | missing doc comments (re-homed) | (none) | nit |
+Promote the exact problems the user complains about — duplicated/copy-pasted code, ignoring shared libraries, needless helper methods, hardcoded instead of data-driven solutions — into first-class FOCUSED VALIDATORS (rules-as-data), so each gets its own fleet agent with an explicit probe mandate. Each focused validator IS one shard/one agent (no "dimension" layer). Today these are buried as Layer-2 footnotes a single agent rushes; the existing `builtin/validators/code-quality/rules/no-hard-code.md` only catches the narrow "return 42 to pass a test" case.

-- Each validator's rule bodies state the carve-outs that prevent over-flagging: `reuse`/`data-driven` honor rule-of-three (two is coincidence, three is a pattern) and no speculative abstraction; `dead-code` exempts entry points, exported public API, and tests.
-- Probe names must be the catalog's (`duplicates`/`similar`/`callers`); NO `search_symbol` or `get_blastradius`. In-file validators declare no probes.
-- Delete the monolithic multi-concern `code-quality` set once its rules are re-homed; `no-hard-code` ("return 42 to pass a test") goes to the `tests`/test-integrity validator (safety-migration task), not here.
+Split the monolithic `code-quality` validator into focused validators under `builtin/validators/` (new no-`trigger` format; frontmatter `name`, `description` = the agent mandate, `match.files` globs, `severity`, `probes`):
+- **duplication** (severity blocker, `probes: [find_duplicates, search_symbol]`): flag verbatim/near-verbatim blocks AND missed reuse of existing shared functions/libraries. Two blocks differing only by a value = one function with an argument. Flag needless helper methods wrapping a single call site.
+- **data-driven** (`probes: [search_symbol]`): flag hardcoded literals and `match`/`if`-chains over a known set whose arms differ only in constants — these are tables, not control flow. Repeated literals → named constant/config. Rule-of-three calibration (two is coincidence, three is a pattern); no speculative abstraction.
+- **dead-code** (severity blocker, `probes: [get_callgraph, get_blastradius]`): added/changed symbol with empty inbound callgraph that isn't an entry point/export/test; orphaned modules, unreachable branches, commented-out code.
+- Each validator's rule bodies state severity, the probe to run, and the carve-outs (rule-of-three, exported APIs, entry points/tests) so the fleet/verifier don't over-flag.
+- Re-home the still-relevant existing code-quality rules (cognitive-complexity, function-length, naming-consistency, no-commented-code, no-magic-numbers, missing-docs) into their own focused validators; delete any made redundant.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] `duplication`, `reuse`, `data-driven`, `dead-code` exist as separate focused validators with the probes/severities above; `reuse` is split out from `duplication`.
-- [ ] The six re-homed quality validators exist; the monolithic `code-quality` set is gone; no validator carries `trigger`.
-- [ ] Every declared probe is a real catalog name (`duplicates`/`similar`/`callers`); in-file validators declare none.
-- [ ] Each validator names its carve-outs.
+- [ ] `duplication`, `data-driven`, and `dead-code` validators exist in the new format, each a single focused concern with `description` (mandate), `match.files`, `severity`, and `probes`.
+- [ ] Existing code-quality rules are re-homed to focused validators; no validator carries `trigger`; the monolithic multi-concern `code-quality` set is gone.
+- [ ] Each validator names the carve-outs that prevent over-flagging.
 - [ ] A loader test confirms the new validators parse, expose their `probes`, and match a `.rs` file.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Loader/parse test: every authored `VALIDATOR.md` + `rules/*.md` parses; declared probes pass `probe_exists`; `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` green.
-- [ ] Fixture review test (engine + scripted agent): a temp file with an obvious copy-paste block, a reimplementation of an existing util, and a hardcoded if-chain → the `duplication`, `reuse`, and `data-driven` validators each produce a finding (proves rule text + probes drive the agents correctly).
+- [ ] Loader/parse test: every authored `VALIDATOR.md` + `rules/*.md` parses, `probes` resolve to real code_context ops; `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` green.
+- [ ] Fixture review test (engine + scripted agent): a temp file with an obvious copy-paste block and a hardcoded if-chain → the `duplication` and `data-driven` validators each produce findings (proves the rule text + probes drive the agent correctly).

 ## Workflow
-- Content-authoring task grounded in code. Read `builtin/skills/review/SKILL.md` Layer 2 and the existing `builtin/validators/code-quality/rules/*.md` first; reuse their wording. Pair each probe-bearing validator with its catalog probe. Depends on the format task (`probes` schema) and the probe registry (catalog names).
\ No newline at end of file
+- Content-authoring task grounded in code. Read `builtin/skills/review/SKILL.md` Layer 2 and the existing `builtin/validators/code-quality/rules/*.md` first; reuse their wording. Pair each validator with the code_context probe that confirms it. Depends on the format task (no-`trigger`, `probes` schema) and the design (validator catalog).
\ No newline at end of file
